data.body:
PASS 1 review (rubric T3 — Integration feasibility [overlay]): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. No findings.

The T3 overlay engages only when a plan integrates an EXTERNAL API/CLI/service/library or asserts a capability it has not used before. Every integration this ISF criterion-build plan depends on is an internal, already-used rebar seam (T3 ANTI-FP: "an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"):

- Frontier-model single-turn LLM call: runs on the SHIPPED provider-agnostic pydantic_ai runtime (src/rebar/llm/config.py); the ISF call itself already exists — pass1_isf() in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py (execution_mode="single_turn", NOT agentic, fed plan + session_log_text). No new external API asserted.
- Eval-suite validation: the Inspect-AI eval seam is SHIPPED (src/rebar/llm/eval.py, .eval.yaml specs, CI-gated, per epic e6d9 / ticket 7284 comments); an ISF eval spec already exists (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml). The eval harness + grader model are owned by 7284 per the relates_to split, not introduced here.
- Linked session-log access + oversized-log SUMMARY fallback: internal rebar infrastructure (rebar session-logs / MCP recent_session_logs; orchestrator.py resolves the linked log; summarize_for_isf() is an internal LLM text call). The summary + reduced-confidence fallback is the plan's own mitigation for the context-window limit — internal runtime input handling, not a new external capability.

No external subcommand/endpoint to verify (MATCH/MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED n/a), no auth/HTTPS precondition newly introduced, and no asserted-but-unverified external capability the plan depends on — so no SPIKE-routing gap. The overlay does not engage.

Affirmations (T3): not-applicable — all integrations are internal + already-used (pydantic_ai runtime, shipped Inspect-AI eval seam, rebar session-log reads); the criterion + summary-fallback are already realized in shipped code, with no new external API/CLI/service/library asserted.
